page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 48 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 architecture, running at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled as a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and the instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores ranking in the top 25 percent. Memory performance is highly competitive, registering top-tier results in PassMark memory tests and strong read-only bandwidth for both small and large blocks. The instance excels in data processing and compression, achieving top-tier status in single-threaded gzip compression and both single- and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by top-tier performance in PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations.

The instance is well-suited for database hosting, static web serving, and data compression tasks. It also supports moderate machine learning workloads, showing strong text generation speeds for 7B parameter models, though it exhibits poor text generation performance for smaller 135M models. The combination of dedicated ARMv9 cores and high-speed local NVMe storage offers a cost-effective profile for resource-dense, general-purpose workloads requiring high memory bandwidth and fast local disk access without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance utilizing the ARMv9 architecture with 48 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, it features 192.0 GB of RAM, a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory bandwidth, alongside exceptional results in database operations, Redis SET operations, and data compression tasks. While text generation for very small models is a relative bottleneck, the instance demonstrates strong text generation for 7B models. This hardware configuration delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for general-purpose workloads, making it highly suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is an ARMv9-based general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is an ARMv9 instance with 48 dedicated cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARMv9-powered instance combining dedicated physical cores with high-speed local NVMe storage for data-intensive and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory with top-tier bandwidth performance
Bundled 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Top-tier performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database hosting, web serving, and data compression
